headline: Diffusion model internals and agentic tooling drove the week as AI bubble fears hit tech stocks.
body: Diffusion research led the rotation: a new holistic benchmark for generative diffusion transformers landed, and a separate analysis argued diffusion models do not need explicit noise conditioning, aligning with breakouts in noise prediction and generative AI. Agentic development surfaced across new tooling, including a malleable Electron coding-agent app, MoEngage's bet on millions of marketing agents, and Hermes Agent's learn-from-anything capability. On the institutional side, the Broward School Board paused an educator AI rollout over privacy and security concerns while the U.S. pressed Meta to submit to AI security reviews. Tech stocks slumped on AI bubble fears.

cited_items.title: DiffusionBench: Towards Holistic Evaluation of Generative Diffusion Transformers
The Geometry of Noise: Why Diffusion Models Don't Need Noise Conditioning
Show HN: Y – A malleable coding-agent desktop app built with Electron
India’s MoEngage bets that the future of marketing is millions of AI agents
Hermes Agent can now /learn from anything
Broward School Board pauses rollout of AI platform for educators amid privacy and security concerns - CBS News
